questions.question: What does VHDL stand for in the context of digital circuit design?
options: Very High-speed Integrated Circuit Hardware Description Language
Virtual Hardware Design Language
Vectorized High-Level Digital Logic
Volatile Hardware Description Library
Versatile Hierarchical Design Language

questions.question: In the physical verification phase of IC design, what is the primary purpose of Design Rule Checking (DRC)?
options: To verify that the synthesized gate-level netlist matches the RTL design.
To ensure the layout adheres to manufacturing constraints and rules for reliability and yield.
To calculate the timing delays of paths in the circuit.
To extract parasitic resistance and capacitance from the layout.
To optimize the placement of standard cells for better performance.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using Direct Memory Access (DMA) in a computer system?
options: It allows the CPU to directly access I/O devices without needing an I/O controller.
It reduces the number of general-purpose registers required by the CPU.
It enables I/O devices to transfer data to and from memory without continuous CPU intervention.
It provides a dedicated high-speed cache for I/O operations.
It simplifies the instruction set architecture by eliminating I/O instructions.

questions.question: In modern System-on-Chip (SoC) design, which technique is primarily employed to dynamically adjust the operating frequency and voltage of different functional blocks to optimize power consumption while meeting performance requirements?
options: Clock Tree Synthesis (CTS)
Static Timing Analysis (STA)
Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS)
Logic Synthesis (LS)
Register Renaming

questions.question: In a multi-processor system, what does a "memory consistency model" primarily define?
options: The maximum amount of physical memory that can be addressed by the CPU.
The set of instructions available for memory access operations.
The rules for when a memory write performed by one processor becomes visible to other processors.
The hierarchy and organization of cache levels within each processor.
The physical layout and interconnects between processors and memory.
